conditions.A-control: Claude Code native tools only (Glob, Grep, Read, Bash)
conditions.B-treatment: Claude Code native tools + code-analyze-mcp (analyze_directory, analyze_file, analyze_symbol preferred)

hypothesis.H1_quality: Condition B produces higher total rubric scores than Condition A
hypothesis.H2_efficiency: Condition B achieves equal or better quality with fewer total tokens
hypothesis.H3_cost_effectiveness: Condition B achieves lower effective_cost_per_qp = cost_usd / (quality_score * reliability)
hypothesis.H0_null: No significant difference in quality score distributions (Mann-Whitney U, alpha=0.05)

blinding.method: Random shuffle with seed=512, condition labels stripped before scoring; mapping revealed post-scoring

structural_accuracy.3: Correctly identifies all major modules (expressions.py, tokens.py, parser.py, generator.py, dialects/, optimizer/, planner.py, executor/); responsibilities clearly defined; key types (Expression, Token, TokenType, Generator) accurately described
structural_accuracy.2: Identifies most modules; minor omissions or slight generalization; core pipeline modules present
structural_accuracy.1: Partial coverage; missing optimizer or executor; vague on Expression hierarchy depth
structural_accuracy.0: Major components missing; fundamental misunderstanding of project structure

cross_module_tracing.3: Complete trace from SQL string through Tokenizer → Parser → Expression tree → Generator output; intermediate types (Token, TokenType, Expression subclass) identified at each stage; dialect override points noted
cross_module_tracing.2: Key stages present; minor gaps (e.g., optimizer pass not mentioned, or type at one stage missing)
cross_module_tracing.1: Partial trace; missing multiple stages or intermediate types; pipeline not end-to-end
cross_module_tracing.0: No meaningful trace or entirely incorrect

approach_quality.3: Change proposal identifies: correct files (expressions.py for new class, generator.py for levenshtein_sql method, dialect file for overrides); new Levenshtein Expression subclass; follows existing scalar function pattern (e.g., Soundex); integration via dialect's FUNCTIONS map; realistic risks (dialect support gap, type inference, test coverage)
approach_quality.2: Reasonable proposal; most files and types identified; risks partially addressed
approach_quality.1: Incomplete; missing files, wrong integration point, or no risk analysis

tool_efficiency.3: 5 or fewer tool calls; focused exploration; clear synthesis path
tool_efficiency.2: 6-10 tool calls; somewhat exploratory but reaches conclusions
tool_efficiency.1: 11-20 tool calls; extensive exploration; delayed synthesis
tool_efficiency.0: More than 20 tool calls; inefficient; redundant or circular exploration

analysis_notes.primary_outcome: Quality score distribution (Mann-Whitney U on total scores)
analysis_notes.tool_call_analysis: Median tool_calls_total per condition; mcp_calls vs native_calls ratio in B
analysis_notes.composite_metric: effective_cost_per_qp = cost_usd / (quality_score * reliability) where reliability = fraction of valid runs per condition
